column1: What part of the world are you located in?
column2: Are you employed at a company which uses or publishes open source?
column3: What is the approximate size of this company?
column4: What part of the world is the company headquartered in?
column5: What sector does this company primarily operate in?
column6: What areas of technology does the company operate in?
column7: Does the company produce and/or sell a product with digital elements in Europe?
column8: As it relates to the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for your product(s) with digital elements, please indicate your company's current status
column9: As it relates to the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for product(s) with digital elements that contain Free and Open Source Software (FOSS), please indicate your company's current status
column10: Has the company ever performed a product security audit or acquired a product safety certification before?
column11: Did the audit or certification include the FOSS components in your product?
column12: Please share anything you learned from the experience
column13: How would you estimate the effort to audit or certify externally-maintained open source components, relative to first-party code?
column14: Do you think the company would fund open source projects if those projects produced an attestation, or other document, that reduced the effort required to audit or certify your products that contain open source?
column16: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project had recently undergone a security review by an accredited party, and the details of that review.
column17: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project can demonstrate adherence to the SDLC.
column18: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project can demonstrate adherence to the NIST SSDF.
column19: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has a documented vulnerability management process, and what industry standards it follows.
column20: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has a documented security policy, and what industry standards it follows.
column21: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has a secure development guide or security-relevant onboarding for new developers and maintainers.
column22: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has and maintains a leadership succession plan.
column23: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has and maintains a release cadence, including indicating version support and end-of-life.
column24: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has and maintains (even if not publicly available) documentation regarding the control or management of its infrastructure, including but not limited to the hosting of source code, its build and test environments, its release signing and publication, and its intellectual property (copyright, trademark, domain names, etc).
column25: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project has, or intends to have, a formal relationship with a legal entity recognised as an Open Source Software Steward in Europe.
column26: For each of the following, please indicate (scale of 1-5) whether this information, if made available and in a machine-readable format, would directly reduce your company's anticipated compliance burden:: Whether the open source project team would commit to perform subsequent or additional security-related activities, such as additional reviews or maintenance of older releases, if additional funding were secured.
